question: Thermal power plants can lead to :

question:
Which of the following statement(s) is (are)
incorrect reason for eutrophication?
(A) excess usage of fertilisers
(B) excess usage of detergents
(C) dense plant population in water bodies
(D) lack of nutrients in water bodies that prevent plant growth

Choose the most appropriate answer from the options given below :
solution: The process in which nutrient enriched water bodies support a dense plant population which kills animal life by depriving it of oxygen and results in subsequent loss of biodiversity is known as eutrophication.

subject: Chemistry
q: Some gases are responsible for heating of atmosphere (green house effect). Identify from the following the gaseous species which does not cause it.
question: Some gases are responsible for heating of atmosphere (green house effect). Identify from the following the gaseous species which does not cause it.
solution: About <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>75</mn><mo>%</mo></math> of the solar energy reaching the earth is absorbed by the earth’s surface, which increases its temperature. The rest of the heat radiates back to the atmosphere. Some of the heat is trapped by gases such as carbon dioxide, methane, ozone, chlorofluorocarbon compounds (CFCs) and water vapour in the atmosphere. Thus, they add to the heating of the atmosphere. This causes global warming. Hence, Nitrogen gas is not responsible for green house effect.

subject: Chemistry
q:
Match List I with List II

  List-I
Pollutant		   List-II
Disease /sickness
A Sulphate >500ppm I Methemoglobinemia
B Nitrate >50ppm II

Brown mottling of teeth
C Lead >50ppb III Laxative effect
D Fluoride >2ppm IV Kidney damage

Choose the correct answer from the options given below
question:
Match List I with List II

  List-I
Pollutant		   List-II
Disease /sickness
A Sulphate >500ppm I Methemoglobinemia
B Nitrate >50ppm II

Brown mottling of teeth
C Lead >50ppb III Laxative effect
D Fluoride >2ppm IV Kidney damage

Choose the correct answer from the options given below
solution:
F- ion concentration above 2 ppm causes brown mottling of teeth. At the same time, excess fluoride (over 10 ppm) causes harmful effect to bones and teeth

Lead: Drinking water gets contaminated with lead when lead pipes are used for transportation of water. The prescribed upper limit concentration of lead in drinking water is about 50 ppb. Lead can damage kidney, liver, reproductive system etc.

Sulphate: Excessive sulphate >500 ppm in drinking water causes laxative effect, otherwise at moderate levels it is harmless.

Nitrate: The maximum limit of nitrate in drinking water is 50 ppm. Excess nitrate in drinking water can cause disease such as methaemoglobinaemia ('blue baby' syndrome).

subject: Chemistry
q: Excessive release of <math><mi mathvariant="normal">C</mi><msub><mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">O</mi></mrow><mrow><mn>2</mn></mrow></msub></math> into the atmosphere results in
question: Excessive release of <math><mi mathvariant="normal">C</mi><msub><mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">O</mi></mrow><mrow><mn>2</mn></mrow></msub></math> into the atmosphere results in
solution:
Carbon dioxide is very important for maintaining the climate which is suitable for our survival.
CO2 has the property of trapping heat from the sun. So higher the level of carbon dioxide, the higher will be the heat trapped. This leads to the rise in the temperature of the earth, resulting in global warming.Therefore, we can say excessive release of CO2 into the atmosphere results in global warming.

subject: Chemistry
q:
Assertion: Ozone is destroyed by CFCs in the upper stratosphere.
Reason: Ozone holes increase the amount of UV radiation reaching the earth.
question:
Assertion: Ozone is destroyed by CFCs in the upper stratosphere.
Reason: Ozone holes increase the amount of UV radiation reaching the earth.
options: Assertion and reason are correct, but the reason is not the explanation for the assertion.
The assertion is false, but the reason is correct.
Assertion and reason are both correct, and the reason is the correct explanation for the assertion.
solution:
When CFCs react with ozone, then it breaks the molecule of ozone gas which results in ozone layer depletion. Ozone layer protect us from harmful ultraviolet (UV) radiations coming from the sun. Thus, ozone holes increase the amount of UV radiation reaching the earth.

The upper stratosphere consists of ozone O3, which protect us from harmful ultraviolet UV radiations coming from sun.

subject: Chemistry
q: Which among the following pairs has only herbicides?
question: Which among the following pairs has only herbicides?
solution:
Herbicides, also commonly known as weedkillers, are chemical substances used to control unwanted plants. Selective herbicides control specific weed species, while leaving the desired crop relatively unharmed, while non-selective herbicides can be used to clear waste ground, industrial and construction sites, railways and railway embankments as they kill all plant material with which they come into contact.

Nowadays, the pesticide industry has shifted its attention to herbicides such as sodium chlorate NaClO3, sodium arsinite Na3AsO3 and many others.

subject: Chemistry
q: The eutrophication of water body results in
question: The eutrophication of water body results in
solution: The addition of phosphates in water enhances algae growth. Such profuse growth of algae, covers the water surface and reduces the oxygen concentration in water. This leads to anaerobic conditions, commonly with accumulation of abnoxious decay and animal death. Thus, bloom-infested water inhibits the growth of other living organisms in the water body. This process in which nutrient enriched water bodies support a dense plant population, which kills animal life by depriving it of oxygen and results in subsequent loss of biodiversity is known as Eutrophication.

subject: Chemistry
q: The higher concentration of which gas in air can cause stiffnes of flower buds?
question: The higher concentration of which gas in air can cause stiffnes of flower buds?
solution: High concentration of $\mathrm{SO}_{2}$ leads to stiffness of flower buds.

subject: Chemistry
q: The possibility of photochemical smog formation will be minimum at
question: The possibility of photochemical smog formation will be minimum at
options: Srinagar, Jammu and Kashmir in January
solution: Photochemical smog is a mixture of pollutants that are formed when nitrogen oxides and volatile organic compounds (VOCs) react to sunlight, creating a brown haze above cities. It tends to occur more often in summer, because that is when we have the most sunlight. The average temperature of Kashmir in January is estimated to be -3.6°C in Srinagar, -11.8°C in Pahalgam, and -10.4°C in Gulmarg as their minimum temperatures.

q: The layer of atmosphere between <math><mn>10</mn><mi mathvariant="normal"> </mi><mi mathvariant="normal">k</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">m</mi></math> to <math><mn>50</mn><mi mathvariant="normal"> </mi><mi mathvariant="normal">k</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">m</mi></math> above the sea level is called as:

solution: Based on fact layer of atmosphere between 10 km to 50 km above sea level is called as stratosphere.

subject: Chemistry
q: The concentration of dissolved Oxygen in water for growth of fish should be more than <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><munder><mi mathvariant="normal">X</mi><mo>¯</mo></munder><mo> </mo><mi>ppm</mi></math> and Biochemical Oxygen Demand in clean water should be less than <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><munder><mi mathvariant="normal">Y</mi><mo>¯</mo></munder><mo> </mo><mi>ppm</mi></math>. <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi mathvariant="normal">X</mi></math> and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi mathvariant="normal">Y</mi></math> in ppm are, respectively.
question: The concentration of dissolved Oxygen in water for growth of fish should be more than <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><munder><mi mathvariant="normal">X</mi><mo>¯</mo></munder><mo> </mo><mi>ppm</mi></math> and Biochemical Oxygen Demand in clean water should be less than <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><munder><mi mathvariant="normal">Y</mi><mo>¯</mo></munder><mo> </mo><mi>ppm</mi></math>. <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi mathvariant="normal">X</mi></math> and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi mathvariant="normal">Y</mi></math> in ppm are, respectively.
solution: The concentration of dissolved oxygen in water is very important for aquatic life. If the concentration of dissolved oxygen of water is below 6 ppm, the growth of fish gets inhibited. The amount of oxygen required by bacteria to break down the organic matter present in a certain volume of a sample of water, is called Biochemical Oxygen Demand (BOD). The amount of BOD in the water is a measure of the amount of organic material in the water, in terms of how much oxygen will be required to break it down biologically. Clean water would have BOD value of less than 5 ppm whereas highly polluted water could have a BOD value of 17 ppm or more.

subject: Chemistry
q: The pH of rain water is approximately:
question: The pH of rain water is approximately:
solution:
Normal, clean rain has a pH value of between 5.0 and 5.6, which is slightly acidic. However, when rain combines with sulfur dioxide or nitrogen oxides produced from power plants and automobiles the rain becomes much more acidic. Typical acid rain has a pH value of 4.0.

Rain water becomes acidic because gases present in environment are dissolved, so it's pH will be less than 7.

subject: Chemistry
q:
Given below are two statements :

Statement-I : If BOD is 4  ppm and dissolved oxygen is 8  ppm , then it is a good quality water.

Statement-II : If the concentration of zinc and nitrate salts are 5 ppm each, then it can be a good quality water.

In the light of the above statements, choose the most appropriate answer from the options given below :
question:
Given below are two statements :

Statement-I : If BOD is 4  ppm and dissolved oxygen is 8  ppm , then it is a good quality water.

Statement-II : If the concentration of zinc and nitrate salts are 5 ppm each, then it can be a good quality water.

In the light of the above statements, choose the most appropriate answer from the options given below :
options: Statement I is correct but Statement II is incorrect
Statement I is incorrect but Statement II is correct
Both the statements I and II are incorrect
Both the statements I and II are correct
solution:
Drinking water has a BOD level of 1 - 2 ppm. When the BOD value of water is in the range 3 - 5 ppm, the water is moderately clean. Polluted water has a BOD value in the range of 6 - 9 ppm. Healthy water should generally have dissolved oxygen concentrations above 6.5-8 mg/L

Drinking water with concentrations of nitrate (measured as nitrate-nitrogen) below 10 mg of nitrate per liter of water (mg/L) is considered safe. Permissible limit of Zn in potable water is 5 ppm.

subject: Chemistry
q: The deposition of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi mathvariant="normal">X</mi></math> and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi mathvariant="normal">Y</mi></math> on ground surfaces is referred as wet and dry depositions, respectively. <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi mathvariant="normal">X</mi></math> and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi mathvariant="normal">Y</mi></math> are :
question: The deposition of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi mathvariant="normal">X</mi></math> and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi mathvariant="normal">Y</mi></math> on ground surfaces is referred as wet and dry depositions, respectively. <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi mathvariant="normal">X</mi></math> and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi mathvariant="normal">Y</mi></math> are :
solution: Wet deposition is the acidic water received through rain, fog, snow, dew, mist, etc. Dry deposition is deposition of wind blown acidic gases and particles on the ground. Ammonium salt in rain drop result in wet deposition. Oxides of Nitrogen and sulphur settle down on ground as dry deposition.

subject: Chemistry
q: Green chemistry in day-to-day life is in the use of:
question: Green chemistry in day-to-day life is in the use of:
options: Large amount of water alone for washing clothes
Liquified <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi>CO</mi><mn>2</mn></msub></math> for dry cleaning of clothes
solution:
Chlorine gas was used earlier for bleaching paper. These days, hydrogen peroxide H2O2 is used with suitable catalyst.

Tetrachloroethene Cl2C=CCl2 was earlier used as solvent for dry cleaning. The compound contaminates the ground water and is also a suspected carcinogen. Replacement of halogenated solvent by liquid CO2 will result in less harm to groundwater. Hence, given statement (4) is correct.

subject: Chemistry
q: Which of these reactions is not a part of breakdown of ozone in stratosphere?
question: Which of these reactions is not a part of breakdown of ozone in stratosphere?
solution:
Chlorofluorocarbon compounds (CFCs) are non-reactive, non-flammable molecules that are used in refrigerators, air conditioners, plastics, and electronic industries. Once released CFCs mix with atmospheric gases and reach the stratosphere, where they are decomposed by UV radiations.

CF2Cl2g→uvCl⊙g+C⊙F2Clg

The chlorine radical released will react with ozone.

Cl⊙g+O3g→O2g+ClOg⊙

The Cl⊙O will generate chlorine radicals again

ClOg⊙+Og→O2g+Cl⊙

subject: Chemistry
q:
Formation of photochemical smog involves the following reaction in which A,B and C are respectively.

(i) NO2⟶huA+B

(ii) B+O2→C

(iii) A+C→NO2+O2

Choose the correct answer from the options given below:
question:
Formation of photochemical smog involves the following reaction in which A,B and C are respectively.

(i) NO2⟶huA+B

(ii) B+O2→C

(iii) A+C→NO2+O2

Choose the correct answer from the options given below:
solution:
When fossil fuels are burnt, a variety of pollutants are emitted into the earth’s troposphere. When these pollutants build up to sufficiently high levels, a chain reaction occurs from their interaction with sunlight in which NO is converted into nitrogen dioxide (NO2). This NO2 in turn absorbs energy from sunlight and breaks up into nitric oxide and free oxygen atom.

NO2 (g)→hvNO (g)+O (g)  A B-----(i)

Oxygen atoms are very reactive and combine with the O2 in air to produce ozone.

O (g)+O2 (g)⇌O3 (g)B   C-----(ii)

The ozone formed in the above reaction (ii) reacts rapidly with the NO (g) formed in reaction (i) to regenerate NO2 . NO2 is a brown gas and at sufficiently high levels can contribute to haze.

NO (g)+O3 (g)→NO2 (g)+A C   O2 (g) ----(iii)

So, the A, B and C are NO, O and O3 respectively.

The maximum limit of nitrate in drinking water is 50 ppm. Excess nitrate in drinking water can cause disease such as methemoglobinemia (‘blue baby’ syndrome).
